injuryUpdates.injuryType: High-grade right ankle sprain requiring surgery — ruled out of the tournament
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Preseason 2026-27 (approx. 2 months) — will miss the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Last appearance before training-ground ankle injury that requires surgery

injuryUpdates.source: CBS Sports, Al Jazeera, Sports Illustrated — May 11-12, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Massive blow to Pochettino's midfield. Cardoso was a first-choice defensive midfielder and the heir-apparent to Tyler Adams' screening role. Atletico Madrid confirmed surgery; recovery targets preseason, meaning he is out of the tournament entirely. Pochettino's first major squad absence ahead of the May 26 roster reveal.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Gluteal muscle strain — minor muscular issue per Gazzetta dello Sport
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Available for Milan's final Serie A fixtures; not considered a doubt for the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Missed May 11 match vs Atalanta with gluteal strain; trained fully with squad May 13

injuryUpdates.source: Sports Illustrated, FOX Sports, Gazzetta dello Sport, ESPN — May 11-13, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Pulled up with a glute strain ahead of the Atalanta match (May 11). Italian press describe it as precautionary, and he trained with the group on May 13. Form crisis continues — still goalless for Milan since December 28, 2025. Not expected to miss the tournament but every knock now is a national-team story.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Returned to fitness following March quad injury — featuring regularly off the bench for Bournemouth

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Entered as substitute in the 43rd minute; Bournemouth now sixth and chasing European football

injuryUpdates.source: Sports Illustrated, Bournemouth match reports — May 11, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Quad injury from March is no longer the headline. Adams is regularly featuring off the bench but has not reclaimed a full 90-minute Premier League start, so minutes-load is the lingering concern. Promoted in importance now that Cardoso is out of the tournament — Adams becomes the only natural defensive midfielder of his profile in the picture.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Severe lack of minutes — still without a Bundesliga start since December 19, 2025

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Entered as substitute; no starts since December 19, 2025

injuryUpdates.notes: Club correction: Reyna is at Borussia Monchengladbach (permanent transfer from Dortmund, August 2025) — the previous entry listed Dortmund. Situation has not improved at the new club: he has not started a league match since December 19. Roster inclusion remains in genuine doubt entering the May 26 reveal.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Hamstring strain — returned to action after nearly two months out
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned May 9; building match fitness with two Eredivisie matches remaining

lastMatchPlayed.notes: First minutes since March 7 hamstring injury — played 30 minutes from the 59th in a 2-2 draw

injuryUpdates.source: Goal.com, Yahoo Sports, Sports Illustrated, ESPN — May 4-11, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Returned from a March 7 hamstring injury in PSV's draw at Ajax. Pochettino is expected to push for Dest minutes in the Senegal (May 31) and/or Germany (June 6) tuneups. First-choice right-back if fit, but minutes-load is a clear concern — listed as a doubt until he completes a 90.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Undisclosed injury — missed Marseille's May 10 fixture vs Le Havre
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Potentially available for Ligue 1 season finale vs Rennes

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Out of training May 8; missed May 10 match vs Le Havre

injuryUpdates.notes: Marseille have not disclosed the injury. Weah is the starting right-winger in Pochettino's preferred front three — his availability for the Senegal and Germany friendlies (and the tournament opener) is now a watch-item.

injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Ruled out for remainder of Ligue 1 season; international availability unclear

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Shut down for the rest of the season after muscle strain; severity not disclosed

injuryUpdates.notes: Tessmann was on the midfield bubble even before this. With Cardoso out, every fit midfield body matters — but Lyon shutting him down for the season is a negative signal heading into the May 26 roster reveal.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Groin/adductor injury — returned May 9 after 4.5-match absence
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned to MLS action May 9; building match sharpness

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Returned from 4.5-match groin absence and contributed an assist

injuryUpdates.notes: 38-year-old central-defender mainstay under Pochettino. Back fit after a groin setback. Given the Carter-Vickers long-term injury and Miles Robinson's own groin issue, Ream's availability is load-bearing for the backline.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Groin injury — recurrence of issue that kept him out of March friendlies
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Targeting return before May 26 roster reveal

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Missed three consecutive matches with groin issue; only 64 minutes logged in six weeks across ailments and a red card

injuryUpdates.notes: Recurring groin issue is a serious concern — same injury that sidelined him for the March Belgium and Portugal friendlies. Backline depth is the team's biggest worry going into the roster reveal.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Achilles tendon injury — ruled out of the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Achilles injury rules him out of the tournament

injuryUpdates.notes: Agyemang was a depth striker option who scored in the March 2025 Nations League window. Achilles diagnosis ends his tournament hopes. Less impactful than Cardoso but removes a backup-9 option behind Balogun.

injuryUpdates.injuryType: Broken neck — ruled out of the tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Broken neck rules him out of the tournament

injuryUpdates.notes: Son of former USMNT boss Jurgen Klinsmann and a depth GK option. Severe neck injury ends his tournament hopes. GK rotation now narrows further around Matt Turner and the Patrick Schulte / Diego Kochen / Ethan Horvath group.

squadChanges.details: Forced out of the tournament squad by a high-grade right ankle sprain requiring surgery at Atletico Madrid. Pochettino's first major squad absence and a structural hole in the defensive midfield rotation.
Achilles tendon injury rules him out of the tournament; depth striker option behind Balogun no longer available.
Broken neck rules him out of the tournament; removes a depth goalkeeper option.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: Aggregated from bet365 / DraftKings / Sports Interaction via Covers.com and bookmakersreview.com — May 13-14, 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Co-11th choice to win the tournament alongside Colombia. +135 to +150 to win Group D (still favored ahead of Turkey at roughly +165 and Paraguay at +375). Heavy -840 favorites simply to advance from the group. Odds reflect home-field advantage but also a meaningful re-rate after Turkey replaced an easier opponent in the group.

coachUpdate.notes: Pochettino remains in charge and is scheduled to reveal the final 26-player squad live on FOX from Pier 17 in New York City at 3pm ET on May 26, 2026.

summary: The single biggest development since April 16 is Johnny Cardoso's high-grade ankle sprain requiring surgery — the Atletico Madrid defensive midfielder is out of the tournament entirely, gutting Pochettino's midfield depth ten days before the May 26 squad reveal. The USMNT injury list has otherwise grown alarmingly: Pulisic (glute), Weah (undisclosed), Tessmann (season-ending muscle strain), Miles Robinson (recurring groin), plus tournament-ending injuries for depth options Patrick Agyemang (Achilles) and Jonathan Klinsmann (broken neck). On the positive side, Sergino Dest returned from a two-month hamstring layoff at PSV on May 9 and Tim Ream is back fit at Charlotte; no international matches have been played since April 16, so recent form is unchanged ahead of the Senegal (May 31) and Germany (June 6) tuneups.
